introHtml: React Native interviews combine React knowledge with mobile platform specifics. Key topics are the JavaScript bridge, the new Fabric/JSI architecture, navigation patterns, performance on low-end devices, and platform-specific styling. Expo vs bare workflow tradeoffs come up frequently.

topics.bodyHtml:
Old Architecture

JavaScript Thread → Bridge (async, JSON-serialized messages) → Native Thread. The bridge was a bottleneck: async only, serialization overhead, can't share memory.

New Architecture (React Native 0.68+)

  • JSI (JavaScript Interface): Direct C++ binding. JS can hold references to native objects and call methods synchronously without serialization.
  • Fabric: New rendering system. C++ shadow tree, synchronous layout measurement, concurrent features support.
  • TurboModules: Lazy-loaded native modules via JSI. Only loaded when first used.
  • Codegen: TypeScript/Flow specs generate native code automatically.

Hermes Engine

Bytecode-compiled JS engine optimized for React Native. Faster startup (pre-compiled), lower memory, built-in debugger. Default since RN 0.70.

Yoga Layout Engine

Cross-platform C++ Flexbox implementation used to calculate layouts. Runs on native thread. Implements a subset of CSS Flexbox.

FeatureExpo ManagedExpo BareReact Native CLI
Native code accessLimited (Expo SDK)FullFull
Build setupNone (EAS)Xcode/Android StudioXcode/Android Studio
OTA updatesYes (Expo Updates)YesManual
Best forQuick start, most appsMost apps needing nativeFull native control
// Metro bundler: JS bundler for React Native
// Handles: module resolution, transformation, hot reloading
// Config: metro.config.js

// Expo Go: sandboxed app to preview managed workflow apps
// Development builds: full native app with Expo modules
// Required for: custom native code, debugging native modules

import { NavigationContainer } from "@react-navigation/native";
import { createNativeStackNavigator } from "@react-navigation/native-stack";
import { createBottomTabNavigator } from "@react-navigation/bottom-tabs";

const Stack = createNativeStackNavigator();
const Tab = createBottomTabNavigator();

function App() {
  return (
    <NavigationContainer>
      <Tab.Navigator>
        <Tab.Screen name="Home" component={HomeStack} />
        <Tab.Screen name="Profile" component={ProfileScreen} />
      </Tab.Navigator>
    </NavigationContainer>
  );
}

// Navigating
const navigation = useNavigation();
navigation.navigate("Details", { id: 123 });
navigation.goBack();
navigation.replace("Login"); // replace current screen

// Reading params
const route = useRoute();
const { id } = route.params;

// Focus effect (runs when screen comes into focus)
useFocusEffect(useCallback(() => {
  fetchData();
  return () => cleanup();
}, []));

// Deep linking config
const linking = {
  prefixes: ["myapp://", "https://myapp.com"],
  config: {
    screens: {
      Home: "", Profile: "user/:id", Details: "post/:postId"
    }
  }
};

import { StyleSheet, Platform } from "react-native";

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flex: 1,
    backgroundColor: "#fff",
    // No px units — density-independent pixels by default
    padding: 16,
    // No shorthand: use paddingHorizontal, paddingVertical
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
  },
  shadow: {
    // iOS shadow
    shadowColor: "#000",
    shadowOffset: { width: 0, height: 2 },
    shadowOpacity: 0.25,
    shadowRadius: 4,
    // Android shadow
    elevation: 5,
  }
});

// Platform-specific styles
const platformStyle = {
  ...Platform.select({
    ios:     { fontFamily: "SF Pro" },
    android: { fontFamily: "Roboto" },
    default: { fontFamily: "system-ui" }
  })
};

// No CSS cascade — each component must have its own styles
// No CSS inheritance — font size set on View doesn't apply to Text inside
// Exception: Text inside Text does inherit styles

// View: like div — layout container
// Text: all text must be inside Text (no bare strings)
// Image: local or remote
<Image source={{ uri: "https://..." }} style={{ width: 100, height: 100 }} />
<Image source={require("./local.png")} />

// ScrollView vs FlatList
// ScrollView: renders ALL children immediately — good for small lists
// FlatList: virtualizes — only renders visible items
<FlatList
  data={items}
  keyExtractor={item => item.id.toString()}
  renderItem={({ item }) => <ItemCard item={item} />}
  ItemSeparatorComponent={() => <View style={styles.separator} />}
  ListEmptyComponent={<Text>No items</Text>}
  onEndReached={loadMore}
  onEndReachedThreshold={0.5}
/>

// Touchable components
<TouchableOpacity onPress={handlePress} activeOpacity={0.7}>
  <Text>Press me</Text>
</TouchableOpacity>

// Pressable: more control (preferred)
<Pressable
  onPress={handlePress}
  style={({ pressed }) => [styles.btn, pressed && styles.pressed]}
>
  <Text>Press</Text>
</Pressable>

// TextInput keyboard handling
<TextInput
  onChangeText={setValue}
  value={value}
  returnKeyType="done"
  onSubmitEditing={handleSubmit}
  blurOnSubmit={true}
  keyboardType="email-address"
  autoCapitalize="none"
/>

// useNativeDriver: run animations on native thread (avoids JS bridge)
Animated.timing(opacity, {
  toValue: 1,
  duration: 300,
  useNativeDriver: true, // only for transform, opacity — NOT layout props
}).start();

// InteractionManager: defer expensive work until animations complete
InteractionManager.runAfterInteractions(() => {
  fetchHeavyData(); // runs after animation
});

// FlatList performance
const renderItem = useCallback(({ item }) => (
  <ItemCard item={item} />
), []); // stable reference — important for FlatList

const keyExtractor = useCallback(item => item.id, []);

// Image caching
// Use expo-image or @d11/react-native-fast-image for better caching
import { Image } from "expo-image";
<Image source={{ uri }} contentFit="cover" transition={200} />

// Expo Modules API (recommended for new modules)
// my-module/src/MyModule.ts
import { NativeModule, requireNativeModule } from "expo-modules-core";
const MyModule = requireNativeModule("MyModule");
export function doSomethingNative(): Promise<string> {
  return MyModule.doSomething();
}

// When to write a native module:
// - Platform API not in Expo SDK
// - Performance-critical code (image processing, crypto)
// - Existing native SDK integration

// TurboModules (new arch): synchronous or async via JSI
// Legacy Modules (old arch): async JSON bridge only

keyTakeaways.title: ⚡ Key Takeaways
itemsHtml: Old architecture: JS thread communicates with native via serialized bridge — the main performance bottleneck
New architecture (Fabric + JSI): synchronous native calls via C++ host objects, no serialization overhead
FlatList virtualizes off-screen items; never use a plain ScrollView for long lists
Styles are inline JS objects resolved to native constraints — no cascade, no specificity rules
React Navigation stack navigators preserve state; tab navigators mount all screens on initialization
Platform.OS and platform-specific file extensions (.ios.js / .android.js) are the two branching strategies
InteractionManager.runAfterInteractions defers heavy work until after animations complete
